| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| Passwords/passphrases for service providers'<br>customers cannot be used indefinitely.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| Applicability Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| This requirement applies only when the entity being<br>assessed is a service provider.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | include device integrity, location, access times,<br>and the resources accessed to determine in real<br>time whether an account can be granted access                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| This requirement does not apply to accounts of<br>consumer users accessing their own payment card<br>information.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | to a requested resource. In this way,<br>access can<br>be denied and accounts blocked if it<br>is suspected<br>that account credentials have been compromised.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| This requirement is a best practice until 31 March<br>2025, after which it will be required and must be<br>fully considered during a PCI DSS assessment.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Further Information<br>For information about using dynamic analysis to<br>manage user access to resources, refer to<br>NIST                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| Until this requirement is effective<br>on 31 March 2025,<br>service providers may meet either<br>Requirement<br>8.3.10 or 8.3.10.1.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | SP 800-207 Zero Trust Architecture.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |

![](_page_183_Picture_0.jpeg)

| Requirements and Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Where authentication factors<br>such as<br>8.3.11<br>physical or logical security tokens, smart cards, or<br>certificates are used:<br>•<br>Factors are assigned to an individual user and<br>not shared among multiple users.<br>•<br>Physical and/or logical controls ensure only the<br>intended user can use that factor to gain access. | Examine authentication policies and<br>8.3.11.a<br>procedures to verify that procedures for using<br>authentication factors such as physical security<br>tokens, smart cards, and certificates are defined<br>and include all elements specified in this<br>requirement.<br>8.3.11.b<br>Interview security personnel to verify | If multiple users can use<br>authentication factors<br>such as tokens, smart cards, and certificates, it<br>may be impossible to identify the individual using<br>the authentication mechanism.<br>Good<br>Practice<br>Having physical and/or logical controls (for<br>example, a PIN, biometric data, or a password) to<br>uniquely authenticate the user of the account<br>will |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | authentication factors are assigned to an individual<br>user and not shared among multiple users.                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | prevent unauthorized users from gaining access<br>to the user account through use of a shared<br>authentication factor.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | 8.3.11.c<br>Examine system configuration settings<br>and/or observe physical controls, as applicable, to<br>verify that controls are implemented to ensure only<br>the intended user can use that factor to gain                                                                                                               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| An authentication factor cannot be used by anyone<br>other than the user to which it is assigned.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | access.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |

| Requirements and Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                       | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |  |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--|
| 8.4 Multi-factor authentication                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | (MFA) is implemented to secure access into the CDE.                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |  |
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                   | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |  |
| 8.4.1<br>MFA is implemented for all non-console<br>access into the CDE for personnel with<br>administrative access.                                                                                                                                                                                      | 8.4.1.a<br>Examine network and/or system<br>configurations to verify MFA is required for all non<br>console into the CDE for personnel with<br>administrative access. | Requiring more than one type of authentication<br>factor reduces the probability that an attacker can<br>gain access to a system by masquerading as a<br>legitimate user, because the attacker would need<br>to compromise multiple authentication factors. |  |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Observe administrator personnel logging<br>8.4.1.b<br>into the CDE and verify that MFA is required.                                                                   | This is especially true in environments where<br>traditionally the single authentication factor                                                                                                                                                             |  |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                                                                       | employed was something a user knows such as a<br>password or passphrase.                                                                                                                                                                                    |  |
| Administrative access to the CDE cannot be<br>obtained by the use of a single authentication factor.                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                       | Definitions<br>Using one factor twice (for example, using two<br>separate passwords) is not considered multi                                                                                                                                                |  |
| Applicability Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                       | factor authentication.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |  |
| The requirement for MFA for non-console<br>administrative access applies to all personnel with<br>elevated or increased privileges accessing the CDE<br>via a non-console connection—that is, via logical<br>access occurring over a network interface rather<br>than via a direct, physical connection. |                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |  |
| MFA is considered a best practice for non-console<br>administrative access to in-scope system<br>components that are not part of the CDE.                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |  |

| MFA is implemented for all access into the<br>8.4.2<br>CDE.                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Examine network and/or system<br>8.4.2.a<br>configurations to verify MFA is implemented for all<br>access into the CDE. | Requiring more than one type of authentication<br>factor reduces the probability that an attacker can<br>gain access to a system by masquerading as a<br>legitimate user, because the attacker would need |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | 8.4.2.b<br>Observe personnel logging in to the CDE<br>and examine evidence to verify that MFA is<br>required.           | to compromise multiple authentication factors.<br>This is especially true in environments where<br>traditionally the single authentication factor<br>employed was something a user knows such as a        |
| Access into the CDE cannot be obtained by the use<br>of a single authentication factor.                                                                                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                         | password or passphrase.<br>Definitions<br>Using one factor twice (for example, using two                                                                                                                  |
| Applicability Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                         | separate passwords) is not considered multi<br>factor authentication.                                                                                                                                     |
| This requirement does not apply to:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| •<br>Application or system accounts<br>performing<br>automated functions.                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| •<br>User accounts on point-of-sale terminals that<br>have access to only one card number at a time<br>to facilitate a single transaction (such as IDs<br>used by cashiers<br>on point-of-sale terminals).                                                                         |                                                                                                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| MFA is required for both types of access specified in<br>Requirements 8.4.2 and 8.4.3.<br>Therefore,<br>applying<br>MFA to one type of access does not replace the<br>need to apply another instance of MFA to the other<br>type of access. If an individual first connects to the |                                                                                                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| entity's network via remote access, and then later<br>initiates a connection into the CDE from within the<br>network, per this requirement the individual would<br>authenticate using MFA twice, once when                                                                         |                                                                                                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| connecting via remote access to the entity's network<br>and once when connecting via non-console<br>administrative access<br>from the entity's network into<br>the CDE.                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| (continued on next page)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

{186}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_186_Picture_0.jpeg)

| Requirements and Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Guidance |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------|
| The MFA requirements apply for all types of system<br>components, including cloud, hosted systems, and<br>on-premises applications, network security devices,<br>workstations, servers, and endpoints, and includes<br>access directly to an entity's networks or systems as<br>well as web-based access to an application or<br>function.                                                                                                                                                            |          |
| MFA<br>for remote access into the CDE can be<br>implemented at the network or system/application<br>level; it does not have to be applied at both levels.<br>For example, if MFA is used when a user connects<br>to the CDE network, it does not have to be used<br>when the user logs into each system or application<br>within the CDE.<br>This requirement is a best practice until 31 March<br>2025, after which it will be required and must be<br>fully considered during a PCI DSS assessment. |          |

|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|

| and administrators, originating from outside the<br>entity's network.<br>•<br>All remote access by third parties and vendors.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | This is especially true in environments where<br>traditionally the single authentication factor<br>employed was something a user knows,<br>such as<br>a password or passphrase.<br>Definitions                                                                 |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Multi-factor authentication<br>(MFA) requires an                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| Remote access to the entity's network cannot be<br>obtained by using<br>a single authentication factor.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | individual to present a minimum of two of the<br>three authentication factors specified in<br>Requirement 8.3.1 before access is granted.<br>Using one factor twice (for example, using two<br>separate passwords) is not considered multi                     |
| Applicability Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | factor authentication.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| The requirement for MFA for remote access<br>originating from outside the entity's network applies<br>to all user accounts<br>that can access the network<br>remotely, where that remote access leads to or<br>could lead to access into the CDE.                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| If remote access is to a part of the entity's network<br>that is properly segmented from the CDE, such that<br>remote users cannot access or impact the CDE,<br>MFA for remote access to that part of the network is<br>not required. However, MFA is required for any<br>remote access to networks with<br>access to the CDE<br>and is recommended for all remote access to the<br>entity's networks. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| The MFA requirements apply for all types of system<br>components,<br>including cloud, hosted systems, and<br>on-premises applications, network security devices,<br>workstations, servers, and endpoints, and includes<br>access directly to an entity's networks or systems as<br>well as web-based access to an application or<br>function.                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |

| Multi-factor authentication<br>(MFA) systems are configured to prevent misuse.<br>8.5                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                         | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                               | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                              |
| 8.5.1<br>MFA systems are implemented as follows:<br>•<br>The MFA system is not susceptible to replay<br>attacks.                                                                                         | 8.5.1.a<br>Examine vendor system documentation to<br>verify that the MFA system is not susceptible to<br>replay attacks.                                                                                                          | Poorly configured MFA systems can be bypassed<br>by attackers. This requirement therefore<br>addresses configuration of MFA system(s) that<br>provide MFA for users accessing system |
| •<br>MFA systems cannot be bypassed by any users,<br>including administrative users unless specifically<br>documented, and authorized by management on<br>an exception basis, for a limited time period. | 8.5.1.b<br>Examine system configurations for the<br>MFA implementation to verify it is configured in<br>accordance with all elements specified in this<br>requirement.                                                            | components in the CDE.<br>Definitions<br>Using one type of factor twice (for example, using<br>two separate passwords) is not considered multi                                       |
| •<br>At least two different types of authentication<br>factors<br>are used.<br>•<br>Success of all authentication factors is required<br>before access is<br>granted.                                    | Interview responsible personnel and<br>8.5.1.c<br>observe processes to verify that any requests to<br>bypass MFA are specifically documented and<br>authorized by management on an exception basis,<br>for a limited time period. | factor authentication.<br>Further Information<br>For more information about MFA systems and<br>features, refer to the following:<br>PCI SSC's Information Supplement: Multi-Factor   |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Observe personnel logging into system<br>8.5.1.d<br>components in the CDE to verify that<br>access is<br>granted only after all authentication factors are<br>successful.                                                         | Authentication<br>PCI SSC's Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)<br>on this topic.                                                                                                      |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Observe personnel connecting remotely<br>8.5.1.e<br>from outside the entity's network to verify that                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                            | access is granted only after all authentication                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| MFA systems are resistant to attack and<br>strictly<br>control any administrative overrides.                                                                                                             | factors are successful.                                                                                                                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                      |

| 8.6 Use of application and system accounts                                                                                                               | and associated authentication factors<br>is<br>strictly managed.                                                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                                 |  |
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                         | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                     | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                         |  |
| If accounts used by systems or applications<br>8.6.1<br>can be used for interactive login, they are managed<br>as follows:                               | Examine application and system accounts<br>8.6.1<br>that can be used interactively and interview<br>administrative personnel to verify that application | Like individual user accounts, system and<br>application accounts require accountability and<br>strict management to ensure they are used only<br>for the intended purpose and are not misused. |  |
| •<br>Interactive use is prevented unless needed for<br>an exceptional circumstance.                                                                      | and system accounts are managed in accordance<br>with all elements specified in this requirement.                                                       | Attackers often compromise system or application<br>accounts to gain access to cardholder data.                                                                                                 |  |
| •<br>Interactive use is limited to the time needed for                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                         | Good Practice                                                                                                                                                                                   |  |
| the exceptional circumstance.<br>•<br>Business justification for interactive use is<br>documented.                                                       |                                                                                                                                                         | Where possible, configure system and application<br>accounts to disallow interactive login to prevent<br>unauthorized individuals from logging in and using                                     |  |
| •<br>Interactive use is explicitly approved by<br>management.                                                                                            |                                                                                                                                                         | the account with its associated system privileges,<br>and to limit the machines and devices on which<br>the account can be used.                                                                |  |
| •<br>Individual user identity is confirmed before<br>access to account is granted.                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                         | Definitions                                                                                                                                                                                     |  |
| •<br>Every action taken is attributable to an individual<br>user.                                                                                        |                                                                                                                                                         | System or application accounts are those<br>accounts that execute processes or perform tasks<br>on a computer system or application and are not                                                 |  |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                                                         | typically accounts that an individual logs into.<br>These accounts usually have elevated privileges                                                                                             |  |
| When used interactively, all actions with accounts<br>designated as system or application accounts are                                                   |                                                                                                                                                         | that are required to perform specialized tasks or<br>functions.                                                                                                                                 |  |
| authorized and attributable to an individual person.                                                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                         | Interactive login is the ability for a person to log<br>into a system or application account in the same                                                                                        |  |
| Applicability Notes                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                         | manner as a normal user account. Using system<br>and application accounts this way means there is                                                                                               |  |
| This requirement is a best practice until 31 March<br>2025, after which it will be required and must be<br>fully considered during a PCI DSS assessment. |                                                                                                                                                         | no accountability and traceability of actions taken<br>by the user.                                                                                                                             |  |

|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                             | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| 8.6.2<br>Passwords/passphrases for any application<br>and system accounts<br>that can be used for<br>interactive login are not hard coded in scripts,<br>configuration/property files, or bespoke and custom<br>source code. | 8.6.2.a<br>Interview personnel and examine system<br>development procedures to verify that processes<br>are defined for application and system accounts<br>that can be used for interactive login, specifying<br>that passwords/passphrases are not hard coded in<br>scripts, configuration/property files, or bespoke and<br>custom source code. | Not properly protecting passwords/passphrases<br>used by application and system accounts,<br>especially if those accounts can be used for<br>interactive login, increases the risk and success<br>of unauthorized use of those privileged accounts.<br>Good Practice<br>Changing these values due to suspected or<br>confirmed disclosure can be particularly difficult to<br>implement.<br>Tools can facilitate both management and<br>security of authentication factors<br>for application<br>and system accounts. For example, consider<br>password vaults or other system-managed<br>controls. |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Examine scripts, configuration/property<br>8.6.2.b<br>files, and bespoke and custom source code for<br>application and system accounts that can be used<br>for interactive login, to verify<br>passwords/passphrases for those accounts are not<br>present.                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| Passwords/passphrases used by application and<br>system accounts cannot be used by unauthorized<br>personnel.                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| Applicability Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| Stored passwords/passphrases are required to be<br>encrypted in accordance with PCI DSS<br>Requirement 8.3.2.                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| This requirement is a best practice until 31 March<br>2025, after which it will be required and must be<br>fully considered during a PCI DSS assessment.                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |

|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                    | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| Passwords/passphrases for any application<br>8.6.3<br>and system accounts<br>are protected against misuse<br>as follows:<br>•<br>Passwords/passphrases are changed<br>periodically (at the frequency defined in the | Examine policies and procedures to verify<br>8.6.3.a<br>that procedures are defined to protect<br>passwords/passphrases for application or system<br>accounts against misuse in accordance with all<br>elements specified in this requirement. | Systems and application accounts pose more<br>inherent security risk than user accounts because<br>they often run in an elevated security context, with<br>access to systems that may not be typically<br>granted to user accounts, such as programmatic<br>access to databases, etc. As a result, special |
| entity's targeted risk analysis, which is performed<br>according to all elements specified in<br>Requirement 12.3.1) and upon suspicion or<br>confirmation of compromise.                                           | Examine the entity's targeted risk analysis<br>8.6.3.b<br>for the change frequency and complexity for<br>passwords/passphrases used for interactive login<br>to application and system accounts to verify the                                  | consideration must be made to protect<br>passwords/passphrases used for application and<br>system accounts.<br>Good Practice                                                                                                                                                                               |
| •<br>Passwords/passphrases are constructed with<br>sufficient<br>complexity appropriate for how<br>frequently the entity changes the                                                                                | risk analysis was performed in accordance with all<br>elements specified in Requirement 12.3.1 and<br>addresses:                                                                                                                               | Entities should<br>consider<br>the following<br>risk factors<br>when<br>determining how to protect application and<br>system passwords/passphrases against misuse:                                                                                                                                         |
| passwords/passphrases.                                                                                                                                                                                              | •<br>The frequency defined for periodic changes to<br>application and system<br>passwords/passphrases.<br>•<br>The complexity defined for<br>passwords/passphrases and appropriateness of<br>the complexity relative to the frequency of       | •<br>How securely the passwords/passphrases<br>are stored (for example, whether they are<br>stored in a password vault).                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | •<br>Staff turnover.<br>•<br>The number of people with access to the<br>authentication factor.                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | changes.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | •<br>Whether the account can be used for<br>interactive login.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                                       | Interview responsible personnel and<br>8.6.3.c<br>examine system configuration settings to verify that                                                                                                                                         | •<br>Whether the security posture of accounts is<br>dynamically analyzed, and real-time access                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
