| Requirements and Testing Procedures                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                  | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                          | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                              | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| Exceptions and anomalies identified during<br>10.4.3<br>the review process are addressed. | Examine security policies and procedures<br>10.4.3.a<br>to verify that processes are defined for addressing<br>exceptions and anomalies identified during the<br>review process. | If exceptions and anomalies identified during the<br>log-review process are not investigated, the entity<br>may be unaware of unauthorized and potentially<br>malicious activities occurring within their network.<br>Good Practice |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                             | Observe processes and interview<br>10.4.3.b<br>personnel to verify that, when exceptions and<br>anomalies are identified, they are addressed.                                    | Entities should consider how to address<br>the<br>following when developing their processes for<br>defining and managing exceptions and<br>anomalies:                                                                               |
| Suspicious or anomalous activities are addressed.                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                  | •<br>How log review activities are recorded,                                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                  | •<br>How to rank and prioritize exceptions and<br>anomalies,                                                                                                                                                                        |
|                                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                  | •<br>What procedures should be in place to report<br>and escalate exceptions and anomalies, and                                                                                                                                     |
|                                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                  | •<br>Who is responsible for investigating and for<br>any remediation tasks.                                                                                                                                                         |

| Requirements and Testing Procedures                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                    | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 10.5 Audit log<br>history is retained and available for analysis.                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                     | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                | Good Practice                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| Retain audit log history for at least 12<br>10.5.1<br>months, with at least the most recent three months                             | Examine documentation to verify that the<br>10.5.1.a<br>following is defined:                                                                                                      | Retaining historical audit logs for at least 12<br>months is necessary because compromises often<br>go unnoticed for significant lengths of time.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| immediately available for analysis.                                                                                                  | •<br>Audit log retention policies.                                                                                                                                                 | Having centrally stored log history allows                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                                                                                                                                      | •<br>Procedures for retaining audit log history for at<br>least 12 months, with at least the most recent<br>three months immediately available online.                             | investigators to better determine the length of<br>time a potential breach was occurring, and the<br>possible<br>system(s) impacted. By having three<br>months of logs immediately available, an entity<br>can quickly identify and minimize impact of a data<br>breach.<br>Examples<br>Methods that allow logs to be immediately<br>available include storing logs online, archiving<br>logs, or restoring<br>logs quickly from backups. |
|                                                                                                                                      | Examine configurations of audit log<br>10.5.1.b<br>history, interview personnel and examine audit logs<br>to verify that audit logs history is retained for at<br>least 12 months. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                                                                                                                                      | Interview personnel and observe<br>10.5.1.c                                                                                                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                        | processes to verify that at least the most recent<br>three months' audit log history is immediately<br>available for analysis.                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| Historical records of activity are available<br>immediately to support incident response and are<br>retained for at least 12 months. |                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

|                                                                                                                                                       | Requirements and Testing Procedures                                                                                                   | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 10.6 Time-synchronization mechanisms support consistent time settings across all systems.                                                             |                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                      | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                   | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| System clocks and time are synchronized<br>10.6.1<br>using time-synchronization technology.                                                           | Examine system configuration settings to<br>10.6.1<br>verify that time-synchronization technology is<br>implemented and kept current. | Time synchronization technology is used to<br>synchronize clocks on multiple systems. When<br>clocks are not properly synchronized, it can be<br>difficult, if not impossible, to compare log files |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                         |                                                                                                                                       | from different systems and establish an exact<br>sequence of events, which is crucial for forensic<br>analysis following a breach.                                                                  |
| Common time is established across all systems.<br>Applicability Notes                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                       | For post-incident forensics teams, the accuracy<br>and consistency of time across all systems and<br>the time of each activity are<br>critical in determining                                       |
| Keeping time-synchronization technology current<br>includes managing vulnerabilities and patching the<br>technology according to PCI DSS Requirements |                                                                                                                                       | how the systems were compromised.<br>Examples<br>Network Time Protocol (NTP) is one example of                                                                                                      |
| 6.3.1 and 6.3.3.                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                       | time-synchronization technology.                                                                                                                                                                    |

{230}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_230_Picture_0.jpeg)

| Requirements and Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 10.6.2<br>Systems are configured to the correct and<br>consistent time as follows:<br>•<br>One or more designated time servers are in use.<br>•<br>Only the designated central time server(s)<br>receives time from external sources.<br>•<br>Time received from external sources is based on<br>International Atomic Time or Coordinated<br>Universal Time (UTC).<br>•<br>The designated time server(s) accept time<br>updates only from specific industry-accepted<br>external sources.<br>•<br>Where there is more than one designated time<br>server, the time servers peer with one another to<br>keep accurate time.<br>•<br>Internal systems receive time information only<br>from designated central time server(s).<br>Customized Approach Objective<br>The time on all systems is accurate and consistent. | 10.6.2<br>Examine system configuration settings for<br>acquiring, distributing,<br>and storing the correct time<br>to verify the settings are configured in accordance<br>with all elements specified in this requirement.                                                                                                                                                                                   | Using reputable time servers is a critical<br>component of the time synchronization process.<br>Accepting time updates from specific, industry<br>accepted external sources helps prevent a<br>malicious individual from changing time settings<br>on systems.<br>Good Practice<br>Another option to prevent unauthorized use of<br>internal time servers is to encrypt updates with a<br>symmetric key and create access control lists that<br>specify the IP addresses of client machines that<br>will be provided with the time updates. |
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| Time synchronization settings and data are<br>10.6.3<br>protected as follows:<br>•<br>Access to time data is restricted to only<br>personnel with a business need.<br>•<br>Any changes to time settings on critical systems<br>are logged, monitored, and reviewed.<br>Customized Approach Objective<br>System time settings cannot be modified by<br>unauthorized personnel.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Examine system configurations and time<br>10.6.3.a<br>synchronization settings to verify that access to<br>time data is restricted to only personnel with a<br>business need.<br>Examine system configurations and time<br>10.6.3.b<br>synchronization settings and logs and observe<br>processes to verify that any changes to time<br>settings on critical systems are logged, monitored,<br>and reviewed. | Attackers will try to change time configurations to<br>hide their activity. Therefore, restricting the ability<br>to change or modify time synchronization<br>configurations or the system time to<br>administrators will lessen the probability<br>of an<br>attacker successfully<br>changing time<br>configurations.                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |

![](_page_231_Picture_0.jpeg)

| Requirements and Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 10.7 Failures of critical security control systems are detected, reported, and responded to promptly.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| 10.7.1<br>Additional requirement for service<br>Failures of critical security control<br>providers only:<br>systems are detected, alerted, and addressed<br>promptly, including but not limited to failure of the<br>following critical security control systems:<br>•<br>Network security controls.<br>•<br>IDS/IPS.<br>•<br>FIM.<br>•<br>Anti-malware<br>solutions.<br>•<br>Physical access controls.<br>•<br>Logical access controls.<br>•<br>Audit logging mechanisms.<br>•<br>Segmentation controls<br>(if used). | 10.7.1.a<br>Additional testing procedure for<br>Examine<br>service provider assessments only:<br>documentation to verify that processes are defined<br>for the prompt detection and addressing of failures<br>of critical security control systems, including but<br>not limited to failure of all elements specified in this<br>requirement.<br>10.7.1.b<br>Additional testing procedure for<br>Observe<br>service provider assessments only:<br>detection and alerting processes and interview<br>personnel to verify that failures of critical security<br>control systems are detected and reported, and<br>that failure of<br>a critical security control results in the<br>generation of an alert. | Without formal processes to detect and alert<br>when critical security controls fail, failures may go<br>undetected for extended periods and provide<br>attackers ample time to compromise system<br>components and steal account data<br>from the<br>CDE.<br>Good Practice<br>The specific types of failures may vary, depending<br>on the function of the device system component<br>and technology in use. Typical failures include a<br>system ceasing to perform its security function or<br>not functioning in its intended manner, such as a<br>firewall erasing all its rules or going offline. |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| Failures in critical security control systems are<br>promptly identified and addressed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| Applicability Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| This requirement applies only when the entity being<br>assessed is a service provider.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| This requirement will be superseded by<br>Requirement 10.7.2 as of 31 March 2025.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |

![](_page_232_Picture_0.jpeg)

| Requirements and Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| 10.7.2<br>Failures of critical security control systems<br>are detected, alerted, and addressed promptly,<br>including but not limited to failure of the following<br>critical security control systems:<br>•<br>Network security controls.<br>•<br>IDS/IPS.<br>•<br>Change-detection mechanisms.<br>•<br>Anti-malware<br>solutions.<br>•<br>Physical access controls.<br>•<br>Logical access controls.<br>•<br>Audit logging mechanisms.<br>•<br>Segmentation controls (if used).<br>•<br>Audit log<br>review mechanisms.<br>•<br>Automated security testing<br>tools (if used). | 10.7.2.a<br>Examine documentation to verify that<br>processes are defined for the prompt detection and<br>addressing of failures of critical security control<br>systems, including but not limited to failure of all<br>elements specified in this requirement.<br>Observe<br>detection and alerting processes<br>10.7.2.b<br>and interview personnel to verify that failures of<br>critical security control systems are detected and<br>reported, and that failure of a critical security<br>control results in the generation of an alert. | Without formal processes to detect and alert<br>when critical security controls fail, failures may go<br>undetected for extended periods and provide<br>attackers ample time to compromise system<br>components and steal account data<br>from the<br>CDE.<br>Good Practice<br>The specific types of failures may vary, depending<br>on the function of the device system component<br>and technology in use. However, typical<br>failures<br>include a system no longer performing its security<br>function or not functioning in its intended<br>manner—for example, a firewall erasing its rules<br>or going offline. |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| Failures in critical security control systems are<br>promptly identified and addressed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| Applicability Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| This requirement applies to all entities, including<br>service providers, and will supersede Requirement<br>10.7.1 as of 31 March 2025. It includes two<br>additional critical security control systems not in<br>Requirement 10.7.1.<br>This requirement is a best practice until 31 March<br>2025, after which it will be required and must be<br>fully considered during a PCI DSS assessment.                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |

{234}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_234_Picture_0.jpeg)

## *Requirement 11: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly*

### **Sections**

- **11.1** Processes and mechanisms for regularly testing security of systems and networks are defined and understood.
- **11.2** Wireless access points are identified and monitored, and unauthorized wireless access points are addressed.
- **11.3** External and internal vulnerabilities are regularly identified, prioritized, and addressed.
- **11.4** External and internal penetration testing is regularly performed, and exploitable vulnerabilities and security weaknesses are corrected.
- **11.5** Network intrusions and unexpected file changes are detected and responded to.
- **11.6** Unauthorized changes on payment pages are detected and responded to.

#### **Overview**

Vulnerabilities are being discovered continually by malicious individuals and researchers, and being introduced by new software. System components, processes, and bespoke and custom software should be tested frequently to ensure security controls continue to reflect a changing environment.

Refer to *[Appendix G](#page-347-0)* for definitions of PCI DSS terms.

| •<br>All authorized and unauthorized wireless access<br>points are detected and identified,<br>•<br>Testing, detection, and identification occurs at<br>least once every three months.<br>•<br>If automated monitoring is used, personnel are<br>notified via generated alerts.                                                                                                                | Examine the methodology(ies) in use and<br>11.2.1.b<br>the resulting documentation, and interview<br>personnel to verify processes are defined to detect<br>and identify both authorized and unauthorized<br>wireless access points in accordance with all<br>elements specified in this requirement. | component. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Examine wireless assessment results and<br>11.2.1.c<br>interview personnel to verify that wireless<br>assessments were conducted in accordance with<br>all elements specified in this requirement.                                                                                                    | a company's knowledge, it can allow an attacker<br>to enter the network easily and "invisibly."<br>Detecting and removing such unauthorized<br>access points reduces the duration and likelihood<br>of such devices being leveraged for an attack.                                            |

|                                     | For example, performing a detailed physical<br>inspection of a single stand-alone retail kiosk in a<br>shopping mall, where all communication<br>components are contained within tamper-resistant<br>and tamper-evident casings, may be sufficient to<br>provide assurance that a rogue wireless access<br>point has not been attached or installed.<br>However, in an environment with multiple nodes<br>(such as in a large retail store, call center, server<br>room or data center), detailed physical inspection<br>can be difficult. In this case, multiple methods<br>may be combined, such as performing physical<br>system inspections in conjunction with the results<br>of a wireless analyzer. |
|                                     | Definitions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                                     | This is also referred to<br>as rogue access point<br>detection.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                                     | Methods that may be used include but are not<br>limited to wireless network scans, physical/logical<br>inspections of system components and<br>infrastructure, network access control (NAC), or<br>wireless IDS/IPS. NAC and wireless IDS/IPS are<br>examples of automated monitoring tools.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |

| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | If using a wireless scanner, it is equally important                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| Unauthorized wireless access points are not<br>mistaken for authorized wireless access points.                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | to have a defined list of known access points<br>which, while not attached to the company's<br>network, will usually be detected during a scan.<br>These non-company devices are often found in<br>multi-tenant buildings or businesses located near<br>one<br>another. However, it<br>is important to verify<br>that<br>these devices are not connected to the entity's<br>network port or through another network<br>connected device and given an SSID resembling<br>a nearby business. Scan results should note such<br>devices and how it was determined that these<br>devices could be "ignored." In addition, detection<br>of any unauthorized wireless access points that<br>are determined to be a threat to the CDE should<br>be managed following the entity's incident<br>response plan per Requirement 12.10.1. |

| The security posture of all system components is<br>verified periodically using automated tools designed<br>to detect vulnerabilities operating inside the<br>network. Detected vulnerabilities are assessed and<br>rectified based on a formal risk assessment |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | While scans are required at least once every<br>three months, more frequent scans are<br>recommended depending on the network<br>complexity, frequency of change, and types of<br>devices, software, and operating systems used.                                                                                               |  |
| framework.(continued on next page)                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Definitions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |  |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | A vulnerability scan is a combination of<br>automated tools, techniques, and/or methods run<br>against external and internal devices and servers,<br>designed to expose potential vulnerabilities in<br>applications, operating systems, and network<br>devices that could be found and exploited by<br>malicious individuals. |  |

| Requirements and Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Guidance |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------|
| Applicability Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |          |
| It is not required to use a QSA or ASV<br>to conduct<br>internal vulnerability scans.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |          |
| Internal vulnerability scans can be performed by<br>qualified, internal staff that are reasonably<br>independent of the system component(s) being<br>scanned (for example, a network administrator<br>should not be responsible for scanning the network),<br>or an entity may choose to have internal<br>vulnerability scans performed by a firm specializing<br>in vulnerability scanning. |          |

| 11.3.1.1<br>All other applicable vulnerabilities (those<br>not ranked as high-risk or critical per the entity's<br>vulnerability risk rankings defined at Requirement<br>6.3.1) are managed as follows:<br>•<br>Addressed based on the risk defined in the<br>entity's targeted risk analysis, which is<br>performed according to all elements specified | 11.3.1.1.a<br>Examine the entity's targeted risk<br>analysis that defines the risk for addressing all<br>other applicable vulnerabilities (those not ranked<br>as high-risk or critical per the entity's vulnerability<br>risk rankings at Requirement 6.3.1) to verify the<br>risk analysis was performed in accordance with all<br>elements specified at Requirement 12.3.1. | All vulnerabilities, regardless of criticality, provide<br>a potential avenue of attack and must therefore<br>be addressed periodically, with the vulnerabilities<br>that expose the most risk addressed more quickly<br>to limit the potential window of attack. |
| in Requirement 12.3.1.<br>•<br>Rescans are conducted as needed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Interview responsible personnel and<br>11.3.1.1.b<br>examine internal scan report results or other<br>documentation to verify that all other applicable<br>vulnerabilities (those not ranked as high-risk or<br>critical per the entity's vulnerability risk rankings at<br>Requirement 6.3.1) are addressed based on the                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | risk defined in the entity's targeted risk analysis,<br>and that the scan process includes rescans as<br>needed to confirm the vulnerabilities have been<br>addressed.                                                                                                                                                                                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| Lower ranked vulnerabilities (lower than high or<br>critical) are addressed at a frequency in accordance<br>with<br>the entity's risk.                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| Applicability Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| The timeframe for addressing lower-risk<br>vulnerabilities is subject to the results of a risk<br>analysis per Requirement 12.3.1 that includes<br>(minimally) identification of assets being protected,<br>threats, and likelihood and/or impact of a threat<br>being realized.                                                                         |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| This requirement is a best practice until 31 March<br>2025, after which it will be required and must be<br>fully considered during a PCI DSS assessment.                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
