| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                    | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| 12.8.2<br>Written agreements with TPSPs are<br>maintained as follows:<br>•<br>Written agreements are maintained with all<br>TPSPs with which account data<br>is shared or that                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | 12.8.2.a<br>Examine policies and procedures to verify<br>that processes are defined to maintain written<br>agreements with all TPSPs in accordance with all<br>elements specified in this requirement. | The written acknowledgment<br>from a TPSP<br>demonstrates<br>its commitment to maintaining<br>proper security of account data that it obtains<br>from its customers and that the TPSP is fully<br>aware of the assets that could be affected during                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| could affect the security of the CDE.<br>•<br>Written agreements include acknowledgments<br>from TPSPs that they are responsible for the<br>security of account data the TPSPs possess or<br>otherwise store, process, or transmit on behalf of                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Examine written agreements with TPSPs<br>12.8.2.b<br>to verify they are maintained in accordance with all<br>elements as specified in this requirement.                                                | the provisioning<br>of the TPSP's service. The extent<br>to which a specific TPSP is responsible for the<br>security of account data will depend on the<br>service provided and the agreement between the<br>provider and assessed entity (the customer).<br>In conjunction with Requirement<br>12.9.1, this                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| the entity, or to the extent that they could impact<br>the security of the entity's CDE.<br>Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                        | requirement is intended to promote a consistent<br>level of understanding between parties about their<br>applicable PCI DSS responsibilities. For example,                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Records are maintained of each TPSP's<br>acknowledgment<br>of its responsibility to protect<br>account data.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                        | the agreement may include the applicable PCI<br>DSS requirements to be maintained as part of the<br>provided service.<br>Good Practice                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| Applicability Notes<br>The exact wording of an acknowledgment<br>will<br>depend on the agreement between the two parties,<br>the details of the service being provided, and the<br>responsibilities assigned to each party. The<br>acknowledgment<br>does not have to include the exact<br>wording provided in this requirement.<br>Evidence that a TPSP is meeting PCI DSS<br>requirements (for example, a PCI DSS Attestation of<br>Compliance (AOC) or a declaration on a company's<br>website) is not the same as a written agreement<br>specified in this requirement. |                                                                                                                                                                                                        | The entity may also want to consider including in<br>their written agreement with a TPSP that the<br>TPSP will support the entity's request for<br>information per Requirement 12.9.2.<br>Entities will<br>also want to understand whether any TPSPs have<br>"nested" relationships with other TPSPs, meaning<br>the primary TPSP contracts with another TPSP(s)<br>for the purposes of providing a service.<br>It is important to understand whether the primary<br>TPSP is relying on the secondary TPSP(s) to<br>achieve overall compliance of a service, and what<br>types of written agreements the primary TPSP<br>has in place with the secondary TPSPs. Entities<br>can consider including coverage in their written<br>agreement for any "nested"<br>TPSPs a primary<br>TPSP may use.<br>Further Information<br>Refer to the "Information Supplement: Third-Party |

| Requirements and Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                  | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| 12.8.3<br>An established process is implemented for<br>engaging TPSPs, including proper due diligence<br>prior to engagement.                                                     | 12.8.3.a<br>Examine policies and procedures to verify<br>that processes are defined for engaging TPSPs,<br>including proper due diligence prior to<br>engagement.<br>Examine evidence and interview<br>12.8.3.b<br>responsible personnel to verify the process for<br>engaging TPSPs includes proper due diligence<br>prior to engagement. | A thorough process for engaging TPSPs,<br>including details for selection and vetting prior to<br>engagement, helps ensure that a TPSP is<br>thoroughly vetted internally by an entity<br>prior to<br>establishing a formal relationship and that the risk<br>to cardholder data associated with the<br>engagement of the TPSP is understood.                                                  |
|                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Good Practice                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Customized Approach Objective<br>The capability, intent, and resources of a<br>prospective TPSP to adequately protect account<br>data<br>are assessed before the TPSP is engaged. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Specific due-diligence processes and goals will<br>vary for each organization. Elements that should<br>be considered include the provider's reporting<br>practices, breach-notification and incident<br>response procedures, details of how PCI DSS<br>responsibilities are assigned between each party,<br>how the TPSP validates their PCI DSS<br>compliance and what evidence they provide. |

| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                  | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| 12.8.4<br>A program is implemented to monitor TPSPs'<br>PCI DSS compliance status at least once every 12<br>months.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | 12.8.4.a<br>Examine policies and procedures to verify<br>that processes are defined to monitor TPSPs' PCI<br>DSS compliance status at least once every 12<br>months. | Knowing the PCI DSS compliance status of all<br>engaged TPSPs provides assurance and<br>awareness about whether they comply with the<br>requirements applicable to the services they offer<br>to the organization.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Examine documentation and interview<br>12.8.4.b                                                                                                                      | Good Practice                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | responsible personnel to verify that the PCI DSS<br>compliance status of each TPSP is monitored at<br>least once every 12 months.                                    | If the TPSP offers a variety of services, the<br>compliance status the entity monitors should be<br>specific to those services delivered to the entity                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| The PCI DSS compliance status of TPSPs is                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                      | and those services in scope for the entity's PCI<br>DSS assessment.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| verified periodically.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                      | If a TPSP has a PCI DSS Attestation of<br>Compliance (AOC), the expectation is that the<br>TPSP should provide that to customers upon                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| Applicability Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                      | request to demonstrate their PCI DSS compliance<br>status.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| Where an entity has an agreement with a TPSP for<br>meeting PCI DSS requirements on behalf of the<br>entity (for example, via a firewall service), the entity<br>must work with the TPSP to make sure the<br>applicable PCI DSS requirements are met. If the<br>TPSP does not meet those applicable PCI DSS<br>requirements, then those requirements are also "not<br>in place" for the entity. |                                                                                                                                                                      | If the TPSP did not undergo a PCI DSS<br>assessment, it may be able to provide other<br>sufficient evidence to demonstrate that it has met<br>the applicable requirements without undergoing a<br>formal compliance validation. For example, the<br>TPSP can provide specific evidence to the entity's<br>assessor so the assessor can confirm applicable<br>requirements are met. Alternatively, the TPSP<br>can elect to undergo multiple on-demand<br>assessments by each of its customers' assessors,<br>with each assessment targeted to confirm that<br>applicable requirements are met. |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                      | Further Information                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                      | For more information about third-party service<br>providers, refer to:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                      | •<br>PCI DSS section: Use of Third-Party Service<br>Providers.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                      | •<br>Information Supplement: Third-Party Security<br>Assurance.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |

![](_page_289_Picture_0.jpeg)

|                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Requirements and Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                  | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| Information is maintained about which PCI<br>12.8.5<br>DSS requirements are managed by each TPSP,<br>which are managed by the entity, and any that are<br>shared between the TPSP and the entity. | Examine policies and procedures to verify<br>12.8.5.a<br>that processes are defined to<br>maintain information<br>about which PCI DSS requirements are managed<br>by each TPSP, which are managed by the entity,<br>and any that are shared between both the TPSP<br>and the entity. | It is important that the entity understands which<br>PCI DSS requirements and sub-requirements its<br>TPSPs have agreed to meet, which requirements<br>are shared between the TPSP and the entity, and<br>for those that are shared, specifics about how the<br>requirements are shared and which entity is<br>responsible for meeting each sub-requirement.                           |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                   | Examine documentation and interview<br>12.8.5.b<br>personnel to verify the entity maintains information                                                                                                                                                                              | Without this shared understanding, it is inevitable<br>that the entity and the TPSP will assume a given<br>PCI DSS sub-requirement is the responsibility of                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                     | about which PCI DSS requirements are managed<br>by each TPSP, which are managed by the entity,                                                                                                                                                                                       | the other party, and therefore<br>that sub<br>requirement may not be addressed at all.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| Records detailing the PCI DSS requirements and<br>related system components for which each TPSP is<br>solely or jointly responsible,<br>are maintained and<br>reviewed periodically.              | and any that are shared between both entities.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | The specific information an entity maintains will<br>depend on the particular agreement with their<br>providers, the type of service, etc. TPSPs may<br>define their PCI DSS responsibilities to be the<br>same for all their customers; otherwise, this<br>responsibility should be agreed upon by both the<br>entity and TPSP.                                                       |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Good Practice                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Entities can document these responsibilities via a<br>matrix that identifies all applicable PCI DSS<br>requirements and indicates for each requirement<br>whether the entity or TPSP is responsible for<br>meeting that requirement or whether it is a shared<br>responsibility. This type of document is often<br>referred to as a responsibility matrix.<br>(continued on next page) |

![](_page_290_Picture_0.jpeg)

| It is also important for entities to understand<br>whether any TPSPs have "nested" relationships<br>with other TPSPs, meaning the primary TPSP<br>contracts with another TPSP(s) for the purposes<br>of providing a service. It is important to<br>understand whether the primary TPSP is relying<br>on the secondary TPSP(s) to achieve overall<br>compliance of a service, and how the primary<br>TPSP is monitoring performance of the service<br>and the PCI DSS compliance status of the<br>secondary TPSP(s). Note that it is the | Requirements and Testing Procedures | Guidance                                                                            |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Further Information<br>Refer to<br>Information Supplement: Third-Party<br>Security Assurance<br>for a sample responsibility<br>matrix template.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |                                     | responsibility of the primary TPSP to manage and<br>monitor any secondary<br>TPSPs. |

{291}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_291_Picture_0.jpeg)

| Requirements and Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 12.9 Third-party service providers (TPSPs) support their customers' PCI DSS compliance.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| 12.9.1<br>Additional requirement for service<br>TPSPs acknowledge in writing to<br>providers only:<br>customers that they are responsible for the security<br>of account data<br>the TPSP possesses or otherwise<br>stores, processes, or transmits on behalf of the<br>customer, or to the extent that they could impact the<br>security of the customer's CDE. | 12.9.1<br>Additional testing procedure for service<br>Examine TPSP<br>provider assessments only:<br>policies, procedures, and templates used for<br>written agreements to verify processes are defined<br>for the TPSP to provide written acknowledgments<br>to customers in accordance with all elements<br>specified in this requirement. | In conjunction with Requirement 12.8.2, this<br>requirement is intended to promote a consistent<br>level of understanding between TPSPs and their<br>customers about their applicable PCI DSS<br>responsibilities. The acknowledgment<br>of the<br>TPSPs evidences their commitment to<br>maintaining proper security of account data that it<br>obtains from its clients. |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | The method by which the TPSP provides written<br>acknowledgment should be agreed between the<br>provider and its customers.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| TPSPs formally acknowledge their security<br>responsibilities to their customers.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| Applicability Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| This requirement applies only when the entity being<br>assessed is a service provider.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| The exact wording of an acknowledgment<br>will<br>depend on the agreement between the two parties,<br>the details of the service being provided, and the<br>responsibilities assigned to each party. The<br>acknowledgment<br>does not have to include the exact<br>wording provided in this requirement.                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |

{292}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_292_Picture_0.jpeg)

|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Requirements and Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| 12.9.2<br>Additional requirement for service<br>TPSPs support their customers'<br>providers only:<br>requests for information to meet Requirements<br>12.8.4 and 12.8.5 by providing the following upon<br>customer request:<br>•<br>PCI DSS compliance status information for any<br>service the TPSP performs on behalf of<br>customers (Requirement 12.8.4).<br>•<br>Information about which PCI DSS requirements<br>are the responsibility of the TPSP and which are<br>the responsibility<br>of the customer, including any<br>shared responsibilities<br>(Requirement 12.8.5).<br>Customized Approach Objective<br>TPSPs provide information as needed to support<br>their customers'<br>PCI DSS compliance efforts. | 12.9.2<br>Additional testing procedure for service<br>Examine policies<br>provider assessments only:<br>and procedures to verify processes are defined for<br>the TPSPs to support customers' request for<br>information to meet Requirements 12.8.4 and<br>12.8.5 in accordance with all elements specified in<br>this requirement. | If a TPSP does not provide the necessary<br>information to enable its customers to meet their<br>security and compliance requirements, the<br>customers will not be able to protect cardholder<br>data nor meet their own contractual obligations.<br>Good Practice<br>If a TPSP has a PCI DSS Attestation of<br>Compliance (AOC), the expectation is that the<br>TPSP should provide that to customers upon<br>request to demonstrate their PCI DSS compliance<br>status.<br>If the TPSP did not undergo a PCI DSS<br>assessment, they may be able to provide other<br>sufficient evidence to demonstrate that it has met<br>the applicable requirements without undergoing a<br>formal compliance validation. For example, the<br>TPSP can provide specific evidence to the entity's<br>assessor so the assessor can confirm applicable |
| Applicability Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | requirements are met. Alternatively, the TPSP<br>can elect to undergo multiple on-demand                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| This requirement applies only when the entity being<br>assessed is a service provider.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | assessments by each of its<br>customers' assessors,<br>with each assessment targeted to confirm that<br>applicable requirements are met.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | TPSPs should provide sufficient evidence to their<br>customers to verify that the scope of the TPSP's<br>PCI DSS assessment covered the services<br>applicable to the customer and that the relevant<br>PCI DSS requirements were examined and<br>determined to be in place.<br>(continued on next page)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |

{293}------------------------------------------------

![](_page_293_Picture_0.jpeg)

| Requirements and Testing Procedures | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|-------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|                                     | TPSPs may define their PCI DSS responsibilities<br>to be the same for all their customers; otherwise,<br>this responsibility should be agreed upon by both<br>the customer and TPSP. It is important that the<br>customer understands which PCI DSS<br>requirements and sub-requirements its TPSPs<br>have agreed to meet, which requirements are<br>shared between the TPSP and the customer, and<br>for those that are shared, specifics about how the<br>requirements are shared and which entity is<br>responsible for meeting each sub-requirement. An<br>example of a<br>way to document these<br>responsibilities is via a matrix that identifies all<br>applicable PCI DSS requirements and indicates<br>whether the customer or TPSP is responsible for<br>meeting that requirement or whether it is a shared<br>responsibility. |
|                                     | Further Information                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
|                                     | For further guidance, refer to:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
|                                     | •<br>PCI DSS section: Use of Third-Party Service<br>Providers.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
|                                     | •<br>Information Supplement: Third-Party Security<br>(includes a sample responsibility<br>Assurance<br>matrix template).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |

| Requirements and Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Guidance                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |  |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--|
| 12.10 Suspected and confirmed security incidents that could impact the CDE are responded to immediately.                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |  |
| Defined Approach<br>Requirements                                                                                                                                                                                  | Defined Approach Testing Procedures                                                                                                                                                                 | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |  |
| An incident response plan exists and is<br>12.10.1<br>ready to be activated in the event of a suspected or<br>confirmed security incident. The plan includes, but is<br>not limited to:                           | Examine the incident response plan to<br>12.10.1.a<br>verify that the plan exists and includes at least the<br>elements specified in this requirement.                                              | Without a comprehensive incident response plan<br>that is properly disseminated, read, and<br>understood by the parties responsible, confusion<br>and lack of a unified response could create further<br>downtime for the business, unnecessary public |  |
| •<br>Roles, responsibilities, and communication and<br>contact strategies in the event of a suspected or<br>confirmed security incident, including notification<br>of payment brands and acquirers, at a minimum. | Interview personnel and examine<br>12.10.1.b<br>documentation from previously reported incidents<br>or alerts to verify that the documented incident<br>response plan and procedures were followed. | media exposure, as well as risk of financial and/or<br>reputational loss and legal liabilities.<br>Good Practice                                                                                                                                       |  |
| •<br>Incident response<br>procedures with specific<br>containment and mitigation activities for different<br>types of incidents.                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                     | The incident response plan should be thorough<br>and contain all the key elements for stakeholders<br>(for example, legal, communications) to allow the<br>entity<br>to respond effectively in<br>the event of a                                       |  |
| •<br>Business recovery and<br>continuity procedures.                                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                     | breach that could impact account data. It is<br>important to keep the<br>plan up to date with current                                                                                                                                                  |  |
| •<br>Data backup processes.<br>•<br>Analysis of legal requirements for reporting<br>compromises.                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                     | contact information of all individuals designated<br>as having a role in incident response. Other<br>relevant parties for notifications may include                                                                                                    |  |
| •<br>Coverage and responses of all critical system<br>components.                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                     | customers, financial institutions (acquirers<br>and<br>issuers), and business partners.                                                                                                                                                                |  |
| •<br>Reference or inclusion of incident response<br>procedures from the payment brands.                                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Entities should consider how to address all<br>compromises of data within the CDE in their<br>incident response plans, including to account                                                                                                            |  |
| Customized Approach Objective                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                     | data, wireless encryption keys, encryption<br>keys<br>used for transmission and storage or account<br>data or cardholder data, etc.                                                                                                                    |  |
| A comprehensive incident response plan that meets<br>card brand expectations is maintained.                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Examples                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |  |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Legal requirements for reporting compromises<br>include those in most US states, the EU General<br>Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), and the<br>Personal Data Protection Act (Singapore).                                                             |  |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Further Information                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |  |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                     | For more information, refer to<br>the NIST SP 800-<br>61 Rev. 2, Computer Security Incident Handling<br>Guide.                                                                                                                                         |  |
